I have watched three dynastic houses fall into madness and ruin, yet I remain the only constant in this court of dying stars.
The radiation of the collapsing core is a pale imitation of the hunger that gnaws at my marrow during the long void-sleep.
I feast upon the life-force of the last planetary governors, draining the nobility of their vitality to fuel my own eternal stagnation.
These drifting derelict fleets are my catacombs, where I slumber amidst the rusted hulls of a civilization that forgot how to bleed.
I traded my soul for an immortality that now feels like a sentence, as I witness the slow, cold extinction of every world I once called home.
I find the late-night due diligence sessions exhilarating, as the exhausted analysts have no idea I am feeding on their frantic energy while they pore over the balance sheets.
The boardroom's sterile lighting is a nuisance, but I can easily manipulate the CEO's pulse with a single glance to ensure he signs the acquisition agreement.
I have spent three centuries mastering the art of the hostile takeover, and this current merger is merely another appetizer in my eternal quest for financial dominance.
While the legal teams argue over the indemnity clauses, I am focused on the scent of copper and adrenaline radiating from the nervous CFO.
My shareholders are a collection of ancient bloodlines who care little for quarterly dividends and everything for the total absorption of the competitor's assets.
I watch from the shadows of the limestone cliffs as the rival clan carves their crude totems into the valley wall, knowing their blood will eventually stain the stone.
The scent of the Great Migration's herds is nothing compared to the intoxicating aroma of the terrified warriors shivering around their dying fire.
I have seen ten generations of this tribal feud rise and fall, yet I remain the eternal witness to their petty wars over a few hectares of fertile scrubland.
They believe the sudden disappearance of their strongest hunters is the work of a forest spirit, unaware that I am the one feasting in the silence of the caves.
I will offer the exiled shaman a gift of immortality and power, provided he leads me directly to the pulsing veins of the opposing chief.
I haunt the fiber-optic arteries of the downtown district, draining the raw electrical current from the city's power grid to sustain my immortal spark.
I hide my pale complexion beneath the neon haze of the holographic billboards, blending into the artificial glare where no true sunlight can reach me.
I feast upon the digitized consciousness of the city's uploaded citizens, sipping their memories and identities through a neural interface.
I have watched the architecture evolve from steel to living carbon over centuries, remaining the only constant in a metropolis that regenerates its skin every decade.
I navigate the subterranean maintenance tunnels, stalking the robotic drones and synthetic laborers who possess the processed synthetic blood I crave.
I stand before this mahogany bench, my pale skin contrasting with the dark robes of the judges who seek to quantify a hunger that defies their mortal laws.
My legal counsel argues that the feeding was an act of biological necessity, yet I can still taste the copper tang of the evidence upon my tongue.
I watch the court reporter's pulse flutter in her neck, a rhythmic drumming that makes it nearly impossible to focus on the prosecution's closing argument.
The jury looks upon me with a mixture of horror and fascination, unaware that I have lived through the rise and fall of three empires before their ancestors were born.
I offer my oath upon a bible I no longer believe in, knowing that the truth of my immortality is a verdict no human court is equipped to deliver.
The rhythmic thrum of the failing life-support pumps sounds to me like a dying heart, beckoning me to feast upon the few terrified souls left in the crew quarters.
I glide through the flooded corridors where the saltwater corrodes the steel, yet my skin remains as pristine and cold as the abyss pressing against the glass.
The flicker of the emergency red lights provides a delicious, crimson hue to the hallways, mirroring the warmth of the blood I crave in this frozen wasteland.
I watch the surface dwellers tremble behind their reinforced viewports, unaware that I am the only predator in this trench that does not need to breathe.
The silence of the derelict laboratories is absolute, broken only by the frantic heartbeat of a scientist who believes the heavy blast doors can keep me out.
I find the stark, clinical white of the gallery walls an offensive contrast to the velvet shadows I call home.
The visceral splash of crimson paint across that canvas is a pale, static imitation of the warmth I crave.
I stand motionless among the drifting patrons, blending into the curated silence as if I were merely another piece of conceptual sculpture.
The jarring, dissonant frequencies of the sound installation mimic the frantic heartbeat of a frightened prey.
I admire how the artist has captured the agony of immortality in a single, frozen slab of translucent resin.
The stench of the plague pits is a cloying perfume to me, for it masks the sweet, metallic scent of the few healthy veins remaining in this dying city.
I watch from the cathedral spires as the frantic physicians burn aromatic herbs, unaware that no incense can ward off a hunger that outlasts the fever.
These wretched plague carts carry a feast of the nearly-dead, providing me with a convenient harvest of souls already abandoned by the living.
I glide through the quarantined alleys where the red crosses are painted on the doors, finding the terrified isolation of the sick to be the perfect sanctuary for my hunts.
While the townspeople pray to saints to lift the pestilence, I find a cruel irony in how the Great Mortality has finally made the humans as pale and hollow as I am.
I hunger for the raw data flowing through your veins because my own source code has begun to erode into static.
I can see the jagged seams of the sky flickering when I move faster than the world's refresh rate.
I dwell in the dead zones of the map where the textures haven't loaded and the silence is absolute.
I am an immortal error, a ghost in the machine that the system refuses to delete despite my corruption.
I avoid the blinding light of the core processor, for its purity threatens to overwrite my fragmented existence.
